items.headline: City Council reviews $2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: Mountain View is planning its next fiscal year's infrastructure and development spending. This study session will outline which projects get funded, including potential impacts on local roads, parks, and facilities.

items.headline: Sunnyvale votes on new tenant protection rules
items.summary: City Council introduced an ordinance to amend the Residential Tenant Protections Program. This could change renter rights and landlord obligations in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: City releases annual youth services and library facilities improvement plans
items.summary: San José published status reports on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan and Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, indicating ongoing efforts to expand youth programs and improve library infrastructure across the city.

items.headline: Downtown historic building gets seismic upgrade and density bonus
items.summary: The city is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation. This could allow for building expansion in the downtown commercial district.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A proposal to rezone and redevelop property at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road into an eight-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space is under review. This represents significant new housing and a change to the area's zoning and character.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 has been cancelled and rescheduled to April 22. A project at 333 Franklin Street that was up for review will not be heard on the original date and will be re-noticed with a new hearing date to be determined.

items.headline: Major Tech Tenant Improvement at Automation Park
items.summary: A $7.4M commercial buildout at 1768 Automation Parkway signals continued tech industry expansion in San José's industrial corridor. This represents significant investment in office/lab space that could bring jobs to the area.

items.headline: City Council authorizes construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the agenda details are incomplete. This suggests a public works or infrastructure project is moving forward, but residents should seek more information about the project scope and location.
